objections.description: LEFT JOIN for issuing_organ lacks cardinality protection. If documents:organizations is 1:N, the JOIN multiplies result rows silently, corrupting ranking and recall metrics. The plan assumes 1:1 without verification.
objections.suggested_fix: Use DISTINCT ON (d.id) or aggregate organizations into array, or verify FK constraint enforces 1:1 before JOIN. Add row-count assertion post-query to detect multiplication.

objections.description: 15% latency threshold for adding body_plain is naive. Selecting large TOAST-ed text columns forces heap fetches and network I/O proportional to result set size. For top-k=100 with 50KB documents, this is orders of magnitude slowdown, invalidating the benchmark by measuring I/O bandwidth rather than retrieval algorithm performance.
objections.suggested_fix: Pre-measure body_plain avg size. If >1KB, do NOT select full text; instead add body_snippet (first 1KB) column or use ES for body_hits only. Benchmark validity > field completeness.

objections.description: Mandatory body_plain in schema conflicts with production PG schema reality. If PG stores pre-processed vectors or externalized text, requiring body_plain in adapter violates zero-post-retrieval-hydration rule by forcing expensive joins or table scans.
objections.suggested_fix: Make body_plain optional in schema; update grader to handle missing body_plain by falling back to content snippet or skipping body_hits. Do not force full-text retrieval in PG adapter if storage model doesn't support it efficiently.

objections.description: Schema validation requires identical field keys across backends, but ES returns _source vs PG returns row dicts. Key paths differ (_source.issuing_organ vs issuing_organ). Plan lacks normalization layer specifics.
objections.suggested_fix: Add explicit normalization step in base adapter class that flattens ES _source and PG row to common dict before validation.

objections.description: JSONSchema validation on every result in the hot path adds 5-10ms per result due to Python overhead. For 100 queries x 100 results = 10k validations, this dominates benchmark time.
objections.suggested_fix: Replace jsonschema with dataclass __post_init__ or TypedDict runtime_checkable. Use validation only in debug mode or sampling-based (validate 1% of results).

objections.description: Query uses to_tsvector(content) in WHERE but selects body_plain. If content != body_plain (e.g., content is processed text, body_plain is raw), the grader receives mismatched text for body_hits scoring, causing false negatives.
objections.suggested_fix: Verify column equality: SELECT content = body_plain FROM documents LIMIT 1. If unequal, use content for tsvector AND return content as body_plain (aliased) to ensure consistency.

objections.description: Missing concern: Hybrid adapter merging logic. If hybrid combines PG and ES results, partial field coverage in either source will propagate nulls downstream. Plan doesn't specify hybrid merge strategy.
objections.suggested_fix: Add requirement: Hybrid adapter must inner-join candidate IDs against both source adapters to ensure field completeness before merging, or filter out incomplete candidates.

suggestions.description: Add covering index hint: CREATE INDEX idx_doc_search_covering ON documents USING GIN(to_tsvector(content)) INCLUDE (issuing_organ, body_plain) to eliminate heap fetches if PG version supports INCLUDE.
Add acceptance criterion: 'Grader null-handling verified' - unit test proving grader treats None as 'no match' without exception, before benchmark proceeds.
Add test for JOIN cardinality: assert len(results) == expected_k, detecting row multiplication from bad JOINs.
